{{Quote|Our revolution is closer to success than it's ever been...if we can only stay alive long enough to see it through.|[[Nolan Gillmunn]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Empire: In the Shadows of Their Fathers">''[[Star Wars: Empire: In the Shadows of Their Fathers]]''</ref>}}
The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Jabiim/Legends|Jabiim]] was '''subjugated''' during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] as the climax of a prolonged conflict on [[Jabiim/Legends|Jabiim]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the local Jabiimi rebels, taking place in the decades after the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] ended.

==Background==
[[File:SecondbattleJabiim2.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Imperial forces invading [[Jabiim/Legends|Jabiim's]] surface.]]
Following the Clone Wars, Jabiim was subjugated and used by the Empire as a mining world, with the result of its valuable ore supplies being exhausted. During the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] occupation, a Jabiimite rebel group had been opposing the Imperials for about 19 years without support from other rebel groups.

They relied on stolen and obsolete Imperial equipment and rapid strikes to combat the Empire and their Jabiimi collaborators. Yet, [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was not willing to pull the last of his [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] off the planet. The harder the rebels fought, the tighter the Imperial grip became.

==Pre-battle==
Potential help arrived in the form of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], which had hoped to convince the fiercely independent Jabiimi to join their galaxy-wide cause. They dispatched [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and several other Rebels to help the local rebels, though Skywalker didn't realize he stepped into his father's shadow—or that the name "Skywalker" could earn him a death sentence from his would-be allies. During the Clone Wars, as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] serving the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] had 'betrayed' the Jabiimi, and the locals did neither forget nor forgive.

[[File:SecondbattleJabiim.jpg|thumb|left|250px|[[Derek Klivian/Legends|Hobbie's]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] under fire by Imperial forces above [[Jabiim/Legends|Jabiim]].]]

Upon landing on Jabiim, they were greeted by a furious mob and were taken prisoner by the very people they had been sent to help. Luke tried to find out what exactly his father did many years ago. Meanwhile, the only people from whom they could expect any help—[[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] pilots [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and [[Derek Klivian/Legends|Derek Klivian]]—discovered an Imperial fleet approaching Jabiim with orders to kill or enslave its inhabitants. Among the Imperial fleet were [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|''Acclamator''-class assault ships]], being known for their use as slave ships. This told the two Rebel pilots that the Empire had come to enslave Jabiim.

==The battle==
[[File:SWEMP36-FC.jpg|thumb|right|180px|[[Jabiimi]]'s last stand, the Empire's final assault]]
{{Quote|All troops, prepare for ground assault.|[[Nolan Gillmunn]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Empire: In the Shadows of Their Fathers" />}}
On-planet, Luke Skywalker and his captors joined forces to repel an Imperial assault force of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and immense walker transports like [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] and [[All Terrain Tactical Enforcer/Legends|AT-TE]] walkers.

Luke had a hard time focusing his fledgling Jedi abilities, since he was haunted by the fact that his father, Anakin Skywalker, was not a hero to the people of Jabiim, but a traitor. After the disastrous [[Battle of Jabiim]], Anakin had refused to evacuate the Jabiimi loyalists and as a result, the loyalists were subject to a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] rule of their homeworld.

With superior technology, the Imperials won the battle and crushed the rebellion. However, the Empire also made a deal with a group of wealthy Jabiimi: in exchange for their own freedom, they would give the rest of the planet's population to the Imperials as slaves.

==Removing past mistakes==
{{Quote|It's one of the planets he vowed never to return, fate had other plans.|Commentary on [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Empire: In the Shadows of Their Fathers" />}}
What would complicate things further was the fact that [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] himself was present in orbit above the planet. Haunted by the memories of his former life, he ordered a full-scale razing of the planet, destroying all life on its surface with the excuse that the Alliance would thus be deprived of a potential ally.

In the ensuing chaos, the rebel groups managed to escape and kill their Jabiimi captors, with the Jabiim rebels fleeing towards the nearest mines, now their only means of escaping the planetary bombardment.

Days later, the last mine-turned-fortress would fall to an Imperial ground force led by [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Janek Sunber/Legends|Janek Sunber]]. All remaining survivors were reportedly led to an awaiting slave-transport.<ref name="Empire 36">[[Empire 36|''Empire'' 36]]</ref>
